prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is presented in black and white and appears to move from an animated or graphic opening into a live-action studio presentation.

At the start, the frame shows a simple illustrated/animated composition. Near the upper part of the image there is a small cartoon-like figure or mascot associated with a long, dark, diagonal object that resembles a pen or pen tip. On the lower right, large block lettering is partly visible; it appears to read “NEW,” though the image is somewhat blurred. The background is plain and light, giving the opening a graphic, advertisement-card appearance.

By the next sampled frame, the scene has changed to live action. A formally dressed man stands in front of heavy curtains. He wears a dark suit or tuxedo jacket, a light shirt, a bow tie, and a boutonniere. He holds a small slender object in one hand, likely a pen, though the frame is not sharp enough to confirm every detail. To his right is a framed display board or sign. Only part of the sign is visible, showing cursive letters beginning with “wi…” and larger block letters beginning “PAP…”.

In the final frame, the man remains beside the display board, now more fully visible. The board contains the written message: “without a PAPER MATE pen.” The words “without a” are in cursive script, “PAPER MATE” is in large block capitals, and “pen” appears in smaller cursive writing. A small cartoon mascot or figure is also printed near the lower part of the board. The man gestures toward the camera while still holding the small object, maintaining the demonstrative pose of a presenter.
